notes: WHO reported multiple strikes hit a kindergarten and a hospital in Kalogi; death toll of 114 people including 63 children, with 35 others injured. Paramedics and responders were attacked while moving the injured.
A strike on a major electricity station in Blue Nile State caused widespread blackouts across the capital Damazin, disrupting water and health services.

location: Villages around El Fasher, arriving in Tawila, North Darfur
notes: Insecurity continues to force people to flee villages around El Fasher amid acute shortages of food, water and essential services; nearly 15,000 people reportedly arrived in Tawila in recent weeks, straining host communities.

location: Kadugli locality, South Kordofan / Omran village, Ar Rahad locality, North Kordofan
notes: Renewed violence displaced as many as 600 people from Kadugli locality, while over 600 people separately fled Omran village in Ar Rahad locality, North Kordofan.
WHO reported 1,102 cholera cases and 120 deaths since the outbreak began, with 40 percent of Sudan's health facilities non-functional and the remaining 60 percent only partially functional.
